title: verbascum faurei subsp. acanthifolium
intro: Verbascum faurei subsp. acanthifolium, also known as Mullein, is a biennial plant in the figwort family that is native to the Iberian Peninsula. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and expectorant properties, which can help to soothe respiratory issues such as bronchitis, asthma, and coughs. The medicinal properties of Verbascum faurei subsp. acanthifolium can also provide relief from skin conditions, wounds, and digestive issues, and it has been traditionally used to treat conditions like eczema, acne, and ulcers. Herbal preparations of Mullein can be made into teas, tinctures, salves, and infusions, which can be used to treat various health issues, and it is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
